Enough Said (2013)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $17,550,872Details
International Box Office $8,070,577
Worldwide Box Office $25,621,449
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $2,971,479
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $761,061
Total $3,732,540
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Eva is a divorced soon-to-be empty nester wondering about her next act. She meets Marianne, the embodiment of her perfect self. Armed with a restored outlook on being middle-aged and single, Eva decides to take a chance on new love interest Albert - a sweet, funny, and like-minded man. Things get complicated when Eva discovers that Albert is in fact the dreaded ex-husband of Marianne. Eva tries to secretly juggle both relationships and wonders whether her new favorite friend's disastrous ex can be her cue for happiness.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$232,800 (1.3% of total gross)
Legs:8.00 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:68.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$8,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:4 opening theaters/835 max. theaters, 6.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $25,818,375

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: September 18th, 2013 (Limited) by Fox Searchlight
International Releases: November 1st, 2013 (Wide) (Mexico)
November 7th, 2013 (Wide) (Israel)
November 7th, 2013 (Wide) (Singapore)
November 14th, 2013 (Wide) (Australia)
November 28th, 2013 (Wide) (Argentina)
Video Release: January 14th, 2014 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for sexual content, some thematic material and brief language.
(Rating bulletin 2268, 4/17/2013)
Running Time: 93 minutes

Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Eva is a divorced soon-to-be empty nester wondering about her next act. She meets Marianne, the embodiment of her perfect self. Armed with a restored outlook on being middle-aged and single, Eva decides to take a chance on new love interest Albert - a sweet, funny, and like-minded man. Things get complicated when Eva discovers that Albert is in fact the dreaded ex-husband of Marianne. Eva tries to secretly juggle both relationships and wonders whether her new favorite friend's disastrous ex can be her cue for happiness.
